parsed_review.summary: CP00-409 (RP13.P07.M03.S07-S16, risk A, 10 units, payments core p07 implementation slice) passes read-only review. Scope and deliverable distribution {failure_recovery:6, security_audit:1, implementation:1, hermes_evidence:1, fixture:1} are consistent across closeout-pack-plan.json, review-baseline.json, manifest.json, contracts/payments-core-contract.json, and PAYMENTS_CORE_CP409_PACK_BINDING; plan source_ledger_sha matches. The pack is strictly descriptor-only: every case-set row sets descriptor_only:true/runtime_execution:false/raw_payload_included:false, freezeCp409Result and the CP392->...->CP408->CP409 no-write attestation chain force all dispatch/write/runtime-receipt flags false, and validatePaymentsCoreCp409P07ImplementationSliceDescriptor enforces them. Tests load the real plan+contract (not mocks) and the validator script asserts cp409Coverage.valid, cp409Slice.valid, and deep-equals the contract descriptor projection and no-write attestation. Authority boundary is intact: claude_is_final_approval:false, human_final_approval_required_for_runtime_opening:true, promotes_claude_to_final_approval:false, claims_enterprise_trust_from_local_validation:false; manifest production_ready:false/runtime_ready:false, so nothing is over-claimed. Hermes evidence packet is a deterministic review packet (emits_hermes_runtime_receipt:false), not product runtime. No real data or secrets present (only negative guards). Review-hardening preflight passed (exit 0, 1455-byte valid JSON; not 0-byte/malformed/fenced/tool-call-shaped) and forbidden_review_evidence covers all CP178+ reject cases. Changed-file scope is clean (7 tracked payments files plus the cp00-409 docs dir); unrelated dirty files are preserved and not attributed to the pack. The post-review artifacts (claude-review-result.json, adjudication.md, construction-inspection.json) and post-review gated commands are not yet present, which is the expected implemented_pending_review state and not a defect. No P0/P1/P2/P3 issues found; final enterprise-trust/runtime approval remains a human gate.
